user_query:
I have a small Python script that’s supposed to extract basic episode metadata from my saved recap pages for a reality show. Right now it’s brittle and crashes. Please do a lightweight code review and refactor of scripts/extract_episodes.py so it reliably parses the provided HTML files and produces a clean JSON summary that I can use in my blog workflow.

What I need:
1) Baseline run and log: Run the current script against the provided pages and save all terminal output (stdout and stderr) to output/run_before.txt:
   - Command: python scripts/extract_episodes.py input/pages output/episodes.json
   - Do not change the command for this baseline; just capture whatever happens.

2) Refactor and fix:
   - Keep the same CLI: python scripts/extract_episodes.py <input_dir> <output_json>.
   - Replace the brittle HTML parsing (it currently relies on fragile regexes) with a more robust approach using only the Python standard library (e.g., html.parser or similar). Do not add external dependencies.
   - The parser must tolerate the two slightly different page structures in input/pages/ and extract:
     • episode_number (int)
     • episode_title (string, the portion after “Episode X”)
     • air_date (YYYY-MM-DD if present; otherwise null)
     • contestants_eliminated (list of strings; treat a single list item of “None” as an empty list)
     • standout_quote (string if present; otherwise null)
     • source_file (the input HTML file name)
   - Sort episodes in ascending order by episode_number (then by source_file as a tiebreaker if needed).
   - Handle missing sections without crashing; fill missing fields with null or empty list as described.

3) Produce outputs:
   - Write the extracted data to output/episodes.json with the following top-level structure:
     { "episodes": [ { episode_number, episode_title, air_date, contestants_eliminated, standout_quote, source_file }, ... ] }
   - Re-run the script after your refactor and save all terminal output to output/run_after.txt. The after-log should show a successful run that writes output/episodes.json.
   - Create a brief review summary at output/REVIEW.md (≤300 words) that explains:
     • The root cause of the baseline failure as evidenced in output/run_before.txt
     • The key refactoring decisions you made (e.g., removal of brittle regex patterns, error handling improvements)
     • A short note on how you extract each field from the differing HTML structures

Notes/constraints:
- Process only .html files from the input directory and ignore hidden files.
- Use only the Python standard library.
- Don’t change the input or output paths specified above. Overwrite scripts/extract_episodes.py in place.

Deliverables I will check:
- output/run_before.txt (contains the original error/trace or output)
- A refactored scripts/extract_episodes.py that runs successfully
- output/episodes.json with the correct schema and data extracted from both pages
- output/run_after.txt (shows a successful run)
- output/REVIEW.md with the concise code review summary All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
